title: Maintain the Seattle capture target ledger
purpose: Rank which Seattle sites and site clusters should be captured first based on current robot workflow focus, buyer value, and access realism.
doneWhen: The Seattle target ledger names the immediate top 25, the next 100 expansion buckets, and the long 300-1000 site universe model.
Capture priorities stay tied to current robot workflow demand instead of generic city coverage.
humanGate: Escalate only when a target requires a sensitive operator-lane, rights/privacy exception, or posture-changing outbound motion.

title: Lock Seattle source policy and invite/access-code posture
purpose: Keep Seattle sourcing narrow, truthful, and off the founder lane for routine approvals.
doneWhen: Seattle source policy names allowed channels, disallowed channels, referral rules, and who may issue invites or access codes.
Routine invite/access-code decisions stay with Growth Lead and Ops Lead inside written guardrails.
humanGate: Founder approval only if the policy expands spend, public posture, or channel scope beyond the bounded Seattle pilot.

title: Publish Seattle intake rubric, trust kit, and first-capture thresholds
purpose: Give Intake, Field Ops, QA, and Rights lanes explicit Seattle rules so they can run without founder review.
doneWhen: Seattle rubric covers source quality, access-path truth, equipment/device fit, trust-packet minimums, and approval outcomes.
Seattle first-capture thresholds and trust-kit checklist exist in one operator packet.

title: Build the Seattle capturer prospect list and post package
purpose: Generate the first Seattle supply wave without widening into generic gig-market posture.
doneWhen: 25-50 curated Seattle prospects are named with source bucket, rationale, and next move, or the org explicitly switches to the broader 100-signup path.
Any post copy stays draft-first and preserves no-guarantee capture language.
humanGate: Human review before any public posting or channel expansion beyond the written Seattle source policy.

title: Route Seattle applicants into qualification and approval
purpose: Classify Seattle applicants using the approved rubric instead of ad hoc founder review.
doneWhen: Seattle applicants are tagged by source bucket, approval state, and missing-trust evidence.
Exceptions are blocked with explicit missing facts instead of silently held.
humanGate: Escalate only when the rubric is ambiguous or the application raises rights/privacy/trust exceptions.

title: Own approved Seattle capturers through onboarding and repeat-ready
purpose: Give every approved Seattle mapper one routine relationship owner from approval through onboarding, first pass, and repeat-readiness so the founder is not the default support lane.
doneWhen: Each approved Seattle capturer has a named lifecycle owner for approved -> onboarded -> first capture -> first pass -> repeat-ready.
Routine mapper questions, support, and coaching stay with capturer-success-agent unless they become logistics, QA, rights, privacy, or policy exceptions.
humanGate: Escalate only when routine support exposes a threshold, rights, privacy, payout, or policy exception.

title: Assign Seattle first captures, reminders, and site-facing trust prep
purpose: Turn approved Seattle capturers into real first captures inside bounded thresholds.
doneWhen: Approved Seattle capturers receive assignment, reminder, and site-facing trust steps through the existing field-ops lane.
Travel, timing, and access blockers are explicit on the issue and visible in the admin queue.
humanGate: Escalate only for missing site access, ambiguous permissions, or threshold exceptions.

title: QA Seattle first captures and route recapture decisions
purpose: Ensure Seattle proof assets are real, clean, and ready for buyer proof work.
doneWhen: Seattle captures receive PASS, BORDERLINE, or FAIL with explicit evidence.
Recapture instructions are attached when the first pass is not proof-ready.

title: Clear rights, provenance, and privacy on Seattle proof assets
purpose: Make Seattle proof packs releasable without weakening trust boundaries.
doneWhen: Each Seattle proof asset is marked CLEARED, BLOCKED, or NEEDS-REVIEW with evidence citations.
Policy-setting exceptions route to the human reviewer and founder only when precedent changes.
humanGate: Human rights review for sensitive or precedent-setting privacy, rights, or commercialization questions.

title: Assemble Seattle proof packs and publish 1-2 proof-ready listings
purpose: Turn Seattle captures into concrete exact-site proof assets with a hosted-review path.
doneWhen: At least 1-2 Seattle proof-ready listings or equivalent proof packs exist with exact-site versus adjacent-site labeling.
Each proof pack includes provenance, coverage boundaries, hosted-review path, and next-step guidance.
humanGate: Escalate only when a buyer-visible claim would outrun the underlying evidence or commercial scope.

title: Research Seattle robot-company target accounts and buyer clusters
purpose: Build a real Seattle demand list that matches the proof assets Blueprint can actually show.
doneWhen: 20-40 named Seattle-relevant robot-company targets are researched with facility/workflow fit and proof-path notes.
Exact-site versus adjacent-site proof rules are explicit per target.

title: Prepare Seattle proof-led outbound package and first touches
purpose: Make outbound specific to Seattle proof assets and hosted review instead of generic AI messaging.
doneWhen: Seattle outbound templates lead with one site, one workflow lane, proof-led CTA, and hosted-review next step.
10-20 tailored first touches are queued for operator approval or event-driven send.
humanGate: Human review before any live send.

title: Run Seattle outbound and move serious buyers into hosted review
purpose: Convert named Seattle targets into serious proof conversations without dragging the founder into routine work.
doneWhen: 5-8 Seattle buyer conversations are active with explicit next steps.
2-3 hosted proof reviews are run end to end or clearly blocked with named reasons.
humanGate: Escalate only for posture changes, non-standard terms, or sensitive rights/privacy questions.

title: Keep Seattle buyer threads inside standard commercial handling
purpose: Prevent routine pricing and packaging questions from escalating to founder review.
doneWhen: Standard Seattle quote bands, discount guardrails, and handoff thresholds are documented and used.
Only non-standard commitments escalate above the designated human commercial owner.
humanGate: Human commercial owner approval for standard quotes; founder approval only for non-standard commitments.

title: Publish the Seattle launch scorecard and blocker view
purpose: Make Seattle progress measurable and reviewable without relying on narrative updates.
doneWhen: Seattle scorecard reports supply and demand progress against the launch thresholds.
Missing instrumentation is surfaced as blocked instead of smoothed over.

title: Mirror Seattle execution artifacts into Notion Knowledge and Work Queue
purpose: Keep the Seattle launch runnable and inspectable for humans outside the repo.
doneWhen: Seattle execution system doc is mirrored into Notion Knowledge.
A Work Queue breadcrumb exists for the current Seattle activation state and next human gate.
humanGate: Escalate only for ambiguous Notion identity or rights-sensitive content movement.

title: Run the Seattle switch-on review before activation
purpose: Confirm the software/runtime surfaces needed by the Seattle launch are safe before switch-on.
doneWhen: Seattle switch-on review returns GO, CONDITIONAL GO, or HOLD with evidence.
Any software/runtime blocker is routed to the right engineering lane before launch activation.
humanGate: CTO review on release safety; founder only if compliance or rights evidence is ambiguous.
